# Custom Card

Custom Card displays customizable card content with flexible layouts. It provides a clean, structured way to present headings, snippets of text, and optional images in a card format. Custom cards are designed to be used within a card collection container and support both text-only and image-enhanced layouts.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the custom card's properties in real-time. Test with and without images, toggle featured status, and adjust content.

<Canvas of={CustomCardStories.customCard} />
<Controls of={CustomCardStories.customCard} />

## Component Variations

### With and Without Images

- **With Image**: Displays a responsive image alongside the card content, creating a richer visual presentation
- **Without Image**: Shows only the heading and snippet text for a cleaner, text-focused card

### Featured Collection

The `data-collection-featured` attribute controls the visual treatment of the card collection, affecting spacing, sizing, and layout presentation.

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-custom-card.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-custom-card.scss`
- **JavaScript**: `yds-custom-card.js`

### Accessibility

- Heading level automatically adjusts based on context (h2 or h3 depending on collection heading presence)
- Image links include proper `aria-label` attributes using the card heading text
- Image links are marked `aria-hidden="true"` to avoid duplicate link announcements for screen readers
- All links are keyboard accessible with visible focus indicators
- Maintains proper semantic HTML structure

### Responsive Behavior

- Images use responsive image patterns to optimize for different screen sizes and devices
- Card layouts adapt based on the collection configuration
- Content reflows gracefully on smaller screens
- Touch-friendly click targets for mobile devices

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Keep headings concise and descriptive (5-7 words maximum)
- Use snippet text to provide context without overwhelming users (1-2 sentences)
- Choose images that are relevant to the card content and maintain visual consistency across a collection
- Group related cards together in collections for better information hierarchy
- Ensure card URLs lead to relevant, well-structured destination pages
- Use featured collection styling sparingly for high-priority content

### Things to Avoid

- Don't use overly long headings that break layout on smaller screens
- Don't include too much text in snippets (reduces scannability)
- Avoid mixing cards with and without images in the same collection (creates visual inconsistency)
- Don't use custom cards for navigation purposes (use proper navigation components)
- Don't overwhelm pages with too many card collections (limit to 1-2 per page)
- Avoid generic or unclear link text in headings

### When to Use Custom Cards

- Showcasing featured content, news, or updates
- Displaying program or course offerings
- Presenting research projects or initiatives
- Highlighting services or resources
- Creating visual entry points to deeper content

## Related Components

- **Card Collection**: The container component used to display multiple custom cards
- **Event Card**: Use for event-specific information with dates and times
- **Profile Card**: Use for people or directory listings
- **Text Field**: Consider for longer-form content without the card visual treatment
